text: Hello.
zh: 大家好。

text: This is 6 Minute English from BBC Learning English.
zh: 这里是BBC英语教学的《六分钟英语》节目。

text: I'm Neil.
zh: 我是尼尔。

text: And I'm Beth.
zh: 我是贝丝。

text: In this episode we're talking about twins.
zh: 这期节目我们要聊聊双胞胎。

text: Non -identical or fraternal twins come from two separate eggs and share half their genes.
zh: 异卵双胞胎(或称非同卵双胞胎)来自两个不同的卵子,基因相似度只有一半。

text: More uncommon are identical twins who come come from a single fertilised egg and share 100 % of their genes.
zh: 更少见的是同卵双胞胎,他们来自同一个受精卵,基因相似度是百分之百。

text: As humans, we're fascinated by twins.
zh: 作为人类,我们对双胞胎总是充满好奇。

text: When people discover that someone's a twin, they start asking all kinds of questions.
zh: 当人们发现某人是双胞胎中的一个时,总会开始问各种各样的问题。

text: What question would you ask a twin, Neil?
zh: 尼尔,你会问双胞胎什么问题呢?

text: Oh, probably quite an annoying one.
zh: 哦,大概是个挺讨人厌的问题。

text: Maybe, do you share each other's thoughts?
zh: 也许我会问,你们会分享彼此的想法吗?

text: Ooh, I might ask, if one of you is hurt, does the other one feel the pain?
zh: 哦,我可能会问,如果你们中一个人受伤了,另一个人会感觉到疼吗?

text: Interesting.
zh: 有意思。

text: Well, some common questions for twins include, do you have psychic powers?
zh: 其实,人们常问双胞胎的问题包括:你们有心灵感应吗?

text: Have you ever swapped boyfriends?
zh: 你们交换过男朋友吗?

text: And do you have the same dreams?
zh: 你们会做一样的梦吗?

text: But according to Anand Jagatia, who presented a recent episode of BBC World Service's Crowd Science all about twins...
zh: 不过据Anand Jagatia说——他主持了BBC世界服务《人群科学》最近一期关于双胞胎的节目——

text: If you meet a twin and you ask them one of these questions, don't be surprised if they roll their eyes at you.
zh: 如果你遇到一对双胞胎,问了这些问题中的一个,他们朝你翻白眼也不用惊讶。

text: Ask a twin about psychic powers and they might roll their eyes – a way of showing boredom or irritation.
zh: 问双胞胎有没有心灵感应,他们可能会翻白眼——这是表示厌烦或恼怒的一种方式。

text: But nevertheless, twins continue to capture our imagination and in this episode we'll be finding out why, using some useful new words and phrases.
zh: 尽管如此,双胞胎依然持续激发着我们的想象力,这期节目我们就来探究原因,同时学习一些实用的新单词和短语。

text: And remember, you'll find all the vocabulary for this episode, along with a quiz and a worksheet on our website bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: 记得到我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com查看本期所有词汇,还有配套的小测验和练习题。

text: First I have a question for you, Beth.
zh: 贝丝,我先有个问题考考你。

text: These days, twins are more common than you might think.
zh: 如今,双胞胎比你想象的要常见得多。

text: In fact, one in every 42 babies is born a twin.
zh: 事实上,每42个婴儿中就有一个是双胞胎。

text: But do you know which famous Hollywood star is a twin?
zh: 那你知道哪位好莱坞明星是双胞胎中的一个吗?

text: Is it…
a Jennifer Lawrence b Angelina Jolie or c Scarlett Johansson Ooh, I don't know.
zh: A. 詹妮弗·劳伦斯 B. 安吉丽娜·朱莉 还是 C. 斯嘉丽·约翰逊?哦,我不知道。

text: I'm going to guess Scarlett Johansson.
zh: 我猜是斯嘉丽·约翰逊。

text: Well, we'll find out the answer at the end of the programme.
zh: 好,我们节目最后会揭晓答案。

text: Professor Karen Dillon is the author of a book on twins in American literature and a twin herself.
zh: 凯伦·迪伦教授是一本关于美国文学中双胞胎题材书籍的作者,她自己也是双胞胎中的一个。

text: Here she discusses the good and bad sides of twinship with Anand Jagatia for BBC World Service programme, CrowdScience.
zh: 她在BBC世界服务节目《人群科学》中与Anand Jagatia探讨了做双胞胎的利与弊。

text: Anand Jagatia Being a twin isn't always plain sailing.
zh: Anand Jagatia:做双胞胎并不总是一帆风顺的。

text: Anand Jagatia Yeah, it is nice.
zh: Anand Jagatia:是的,这确实挺好的。

text: There's never that fear because you're going through school together, you're sometimes in the same class together, you're making friends together.
zh: 因为你们一起上学,有时还在同一个班级,一起交朋友,所以你从来不会有那种恐惧感。

text: So all of those milestones of childhood in some ways are made easier for twins.
zh: 所以童年的这些重要阶段,对双胞胎来说在某些方面反而更轻松。

text: But then the older you get, the more you you realise how much people really think of you as a twin rather than as an individual.
zh: 但随着年龄增长,你越来越意识到,人们更多是把你当作"双胞胎中的一个"来看待,而不是一个独立的个体。

text: Twins go through many childhood milestones together.
zh: 双胞胎一起经历许多童年的重要阶段。

text: A milestone is an important stage of development, so a childhood milestone could be something like your first day at school.
zh: milestone(里程碑)指发展过程中的重要阶段,比如上学的第一天就可以算作童年的一个里程碑。

text: But being a twin isn't always plain sailing – an idiom meaning easy and problem -free.
zh: 但做双胞胎并不总是一帆风顺(plain sailing)——这个习语的意思是轻松、没有问题。

text: Many twins find it hard to establish their own identity as a separate and unique individual.
zh: 许多双胞胎发现,要建立起自己独立而独特的身份认同其实很难。

text: Our fascination with twins also comes from the age -old nature versus nurture debate.
zh: 我们对双胞胎的着迷,也源于那场由来已久的"先天与后天"之争。

text: How much of our lives is determined by nature – the genetics we're born with – and how much by nurture – the environment we grow up in?
zh: 我们的人生有多少是由先天(与生俱来的基因)决定,又有多少是由后天(成长的环境)塑造的?

text: Because twins are so genetically similar, differences between them tell scientists a lot.
zh: 由于双胞胎基因高度相似,他们之间的差异能为科学家提供大量信息。

text: Here's presenter of BBC World Service programme CrowdScience, Anand Jagatia, discussing this with Helena de Bresse, a philosopher at Wellesley College, Massachusetts.
zh: 下面是BBC世界服务节目《人群科学》主持人Anand Jagatia与马萨诸塞州卫斯理学院哲学家Helena de Bresse就此展开的讨论。

text: And that's right, another twin.
zh: 没错,又是一对双胞胎。

text: Yeah, you hear about twins that were separated at birth, grew up in different parts of the world, and they kind of have these eerily similar life courses.
zh: 是的,你会听说有些双胞胎出生时就被分开,在世界不同的地方长大,但他们的人生轨迹却惊人地相似。

text: And you think, gosh, like how much choice have I had over the way that my life's turned out?
zh: 你就会想,天哪,我人生走到今天这一步,自己到底有多少选择的余地?

text: Absolutely, yeah.
zh: 完全同意,是的。

text: There's that really famous case of the Jim twins who were reunited at age 39 after being separated at birth, and they just had so many similarities.
zh: 有一个非常有名的案例,就是"吉姆双胞胎",他们出生时就被分开,直到39岁才重逢,结果发现彼此有太多相似之处。

text: It was crazy.
zh: 简直不可思议。

text: They both married someone called Linda and then divorced her for someone called Betty.
zh: 他们都娶了一个叫琳达的人,后来又都为了一个叫贝蒂的人和琳达离了婚。

text: They both named their dog Toy.
zh: 他们都把自己的狗取名叫Toy。

text: It was like ridiculous.
zh: 简直太离谱了。

text: The twins Jim Lewis and Jim Springer were separated at birth.
zh: 双胞胎吉姆·刘易斯和吉姆·斯普林格出生时就被分开了。

text: The phrase separated at birth can be used to describe two people with strong similarities.
zh: separated at birth(出生时被分开)这个短语可以用来形容两个人有着惊人的相似之处。

text: Although in this case, the twins were literally separated and adopted by different parents at birth.
zh: 不过在这个案例里,这对双胞胎是真的在出生时就被分开,并分别被不同的家庭收养。

text: When the Jims were reunited in the 1970s, everyone was amazed at how similar their lives were.
zh: 当这两个吉姆在20世纪70年代重逢时,所有人都惊讶于他们的人生竟如此相似。

text: Anne used the adjective eerie, meaning strange in a frightening, mysterious way.
zh: 安用了eerie这个形容词,意思是诡异得让人感到害怕、神秘莫测。

text: And Helena says it was crazy, using crazy to mean strange and fantastic.
zh: 而海伦娜说这"crazy"(太离谱了),用crazy来表示奇怪又不可思议。

text: Yes, I think strange, mysterious and fantastic describe being a twin quite well.
zh: 是的,我觉得"奇怪""神秘"和"不可思议"这几个词,确实很能形容做双胞胎的感觉。

text: OK, Neil, it's time to reveal the answer to your question.
zh: 好了,尼尔,该揭晓你那个问题的答案了。

text: I asked which Hollywood star is a twin and you said C. Scarlett Johansson.
zh: 我问的是哪位好莱坞明星是双胞胎中的一个,你选了C,斯嘉丽·约翰逊。

text: I did.
zh: 我是这么选的。

text: Which was the right answer.
zh: 而这正是正确答案。

text: Yay!
zh: 太好了!

text: Let's recap the vocabulary we've learned, starting with roll your eyes, to move your eyes upwards to show you are bored or annoyed.
zh: 我们来回顾一下今天学到的词汇,首先是roll your eyes,意思是把眼睛往上翻,表示厌烦或恼怒。

text: A milestone is a significant stage in the development of something.
zh: milestone(里程碑)指某事物发展过程中的重要阶段。

text: The idiom, it's not plain sailing, describes difficult and problematic situations.
zh: 习语it's not plain sailing,用来形容困难重重、问题多多的处境。

text: The phrase separated at birth can describe two people who are unusually similar.
zh: 短语separated at birth可以形容两个异常相似的人。

text: It also refers to siblings who were adopted by different parents at birth.
zh: 它也可以指那些出生时就被不同家庭分别收养的兄弟姐妹。

text: And the adjective eerie means strange in a frightening and mysterious way.
zh: 形容词eerie意思是诡异得让人感到害怕、神秘莫测。

text: And finally, if someone says it was crazy, they mean it was strange and fantastic.
zh: 最后,如果有人说it was crazy,意思是这件事很奇怪、很不可思议。

text: Once again, our six minutes are up.
zh: 我们的六分钟又到时间了。

text: But remember, you can find worksheets, quizzes and loads more resources to improve your English on our website, bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: 不过别忘了,你可以在我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com上找到练习题、小测验以及更多提升英语的学习资源。

text: See you there soon, but for now, goodbye.
zh: 我们网站再见,现在先说再见。
